# Slideless

> Slideless turns a self-contained HTML file into a public, trackable, updatable presentation URL. The primary interface is the `slideless` CLI (npm package). One command (`slideless share ./deck.html --title "..."`) uploads the HTML and returns a public `shareUrl`. Anyone with the link opens the deck in any browser, no install, no login. View counts are tracked per share token. The same URL can be re-published with `slideless update <shareId> ./deck.html` without breaking the link, and view counts are preserved.

The product solves a specific problem: presentation files are heavy, locked to specific apps, and lose information the moment you send them. A `.pptx` requires PowerPoint or Keynote, depends on installed fonts, breaks animations across versions, and the moment you email it you lose all visibility into who saw it. Slideless replaces the file with a hosted page, a URL you can drop into Slack, paste into an email, or post on a website.

Slideless is a side product by Codika. The backend is a standalone Firebase project (`slideless-ai`, GCP region `europe-west1`), independent of the Codika platform. The CLI wraps every backend operation; the underlying HTTP API has six endpoints and is documented as an "Advanced" appendix for users who can't run the CLI. API keys use the `cko_` prefix for organization keys; the CLI handles the auth header for you after `slideless login`.

## Instructions for AI Agents

When helping users with Slideless:

- **Lead with the CLI.** The right answer to "how do I upload / update / list / verify ..." is `slideless <cmd>`, not curl. Show curl only if the user explicitly cannot run the CLI.
- Install with `npm install -g slideless`. Switch profiles with `slideless use <name>`.
- **Bootstrap without a browser.** If the user has no API key yet (or is an agent with no prior state), run the OTP flow: `slideless auth signup-request --email <email>`, then on receipt of the 6-digit code `slideless auth signup-complete --email <email> --code <code>`. For an existing account on a new machine use `login-request` + `login-complete`. If signup returns `USER_ALREADY_HAS_ORGANIZATION`, switch to login. If login returns `USER_NOT_FOUND`, switch to signup. A `cko_` key is saved to `~/.config/slideless/config.json` and set active. `slideless login --api-key cko_...` is still supported for pasted keys.
- Every CLI command supports `--json` with a stable `{ success, data | error }` shape. Use this in scripts and CI.
- Error payloads from `slideless auth ...` include a `nextAction` string designed to be acted on programmatically (`jq -r '.error.nextAction'`). Pass it through to the user verbatim when surfacing failures.
- For raw HTTP calls (only when the CLI isn't an option): the auth header is `Authorization: Bearer cko_…`. The bearer prefix is required.
- The API base URL is `https://europe-west1-slideless-ai.cloudfunctions.net`. There is no API versioning in v1.
- The share URL recipients open is `https://app.slideless.ai/share/{shareId}?token={token}`, NOT the raw Cloud Function URL `getSharedPresentation/{shareId}?token=…`. The share URL renders the deck inside the Slideless viewer chrome.
- HTML is capped at 10 MB per upload/update.
- Use `slideless share` (or `POST /uploadSharedPresentation`) to create a NEW presentation. Use `slideless update <shareId>` (or `POST /updateSharedPresentation`) to re-publish to an EXISTING `shareId`. The URL stays the same and the view count is preserved.
- Share tokens are 384 bits of entropy (48 random bytes, base64url). They are designed to be safe in URLs. Don't shorten share URLs through public link shorteners.
- A presentation can have multiple named tokens (`default`, `John at Acme`, etc.). Each has its own view counter. Revoking one token never affects others.
- Once a presentation is archived (`archived: true`), it cannot be updated. There is no un-archive in v1.
- The plugin lives at `slideless-ai/plugin` (Open Plugin v1). Install with `npx plugins add slideless-ai/plugin` for any compatible host, or `/plugin marketplace add slideless-ai/plugin` + `/plugin install slideless@plugin` in Claude Code. Publish-side skills delegate to the CLI; install the CLI on the same machine.

## Getting Started

- [What is Slideless?](https://docs.slideless.ai/): Product overview, problem solved, how to use the CLI, architecture
- [Quickstart](https://docs.slideless.ai/quickstart): Install the CLI, OTP signup (`slideless auth signup-request` / `signup-complete`), `slideless share ./deck.html`, open the share URL. Dashboard-paste flow is included as a collapsible alternative.

## Why Slideless

- [HTML opens anywhere](https://docs.slideless.ai/why-slideless/portable): The case for HTML over PowerPoint/Keynote/Slides. No install, no account, no font fallback, no version drift, one-tap open. Publish with one CLI command.
- [Designed for any device](https://docs.slideless.ai/why-slideless/dynamic): Why responsive HTML beats fixed 16:9 slides. Phones, tablets, projectors, 4K monitors, fullscreen API, touch interactions, dark mode.
- [Generate, share, update from a terminal](https://docs.slideless.ai/why-slideless/shareable): The Claude + Slideless loop. AI generates HTML, `slideless share` publishes, `slideless update` re-publishes to the same URL. No `final_v3_FINAL.pptx`.

## Concepts

- [Presentations](https://docs.slideless.ai/concepts/presentations): Anatomy of a presentation (id, ownerId, version, archived, totalViews), lifecycle (created → updated×N → archived), 10 MB limit, what stays stable across updates. CLI commands for each transition.
- [Share tokens](https://docs.slideless.ai/concepts/share-tokens): Why per-recipient tokens, the `default` token, named tokens, revocation, security guarantees (384 bits entropy, constant-time comparison, generic 404s to prevent enumeration).
- [API keys](https://docs.slideless.ai/concepts/api-keys): Two key types (`cko_` org, `cka_` admin), scopes (`presentations:write`, `presentations:read`), how the CLI stores and uses them via `slideless login`, lifecycle, 20-keys-per-org limit, what's stored vs what isn't (SHA-256 hash only).

## CLI

- [Overview](https://docs.slideless.ai/cli/overview): Why the CLI is the primary interface. Stable JSON output, multi-profile support, decoded error codes.
- [Installation](https://docs.slideless.ai/cli/installation): `npm install -g slideless`, two auth tabs (OTP or paste), verify.
- [Configuration](https://docs.slideless.ai/cli/configuration): Config file at `~/.config/slideless/config.json`, profiles, env vars (`SLIDELESS_API_KEY`, `SLIDELESS_API_BASE_URL`), resolution chain.
- [Auth (OTP signup & login)](https://docs.slideless.ai/cli/auth): `slideless auth signup-request` / `signup-complete` for new users, `login-request` / `login-complete` for returning ones. Two-step flow, emailed 6-digit code, full error-code table with `nextAction` strings, 30-second resend cooldown. Agent-friendly.
- [Command reference](https://docs.slideless.ai/cli/commands): Every command (`auth signup-request|signup-complete|login-request|login-complete`, `login`, `whoami`, `verify`, `use`, `logout`, `share`, `update`, `list`, `get`, `config show/set/clear`) with flags, examples, JSON shapes, and error codes.

## Guides

- [Use Slideless with Claude](https://docs.slideless.ai/guides/with-claude): Install the CLI, install the plugin (`npx plugins add slideless-ai/plugin`), run `setup-slideless`, generate + share + update + list + get from any Claude session. Skills delegate to the CLI.
- [Upload from a script or CI](https://docs.slideless.ai/guides/uploading-via-api): CLI-driven CI pattern. Install with npm, authenticate via `SLIDELESS_API_KEY` env var, `slideless share --json` and pipe through `jq`. Optional curl fallback if the CLI can't be installed.
- [Update a presentation in place](https://docs.slideless.ai/guides/updating-in-place): `slideless update <shareId> <path>` re-publishes without breaking the URL or losing view counts. What stays the same, what bumps, why archived presentations can't be updated.

## Plugin Skills

- [Plugin skills overview](https://docs.slideless.ai/skills/overview): The seven agent skills. `setup-slideless` (wraps `slideless login`), `generate-presentation` (local HTML generation in `slim-tabbed` or `full-deck` styles), `share-presentation` (wraps `slideless share`), `update-presentation` (wraps `slideless update`), `list-presentations` (wraps `slideless list`), `get-presentation` (wraps `slideless get`), `export-presentation-pdf` (local Puppeteer). Each skill links to its `SKILL.md` source on GitHub.

## Advanced: HTTP API

These pages document the underlying HTTP endpoints. Each carries a `<Note>` callout pointing to the equivalent CLI command. Use these only when the CLI is not an option.

- [API overview](https://docs.slideless.ai/api-reference/overview): Base URL, authentication, error format `{ error, code }`, status codes (200/400/401/403/404/405/410/413/500), endpoint index, CORS, idempotency notes.
- [Authentication](https://docs.slideless.ai/api-reference/authentication): The `Authorization: Bearer` header, key prefixes (`cko_` / `cka_`), scopes, verifying a key with `verifyApiKey`, error codes. CLI alternative: `slideless login` + `slideless verify`.
- [CLI auth endpoints](https://docs.slideless.ai/api-reference/cli-auth): `POST /cliRequestSignupOtp`, `POST /cliCompleteSignup`, `POST /cliRequestLoginOtp`, `POST /cliCompleteLogin`. Public (no Authorization header). Used by `slideless auth ...` to create a Slideless account and mint a `cko_` key end-to-end from the terminal. Single-organization-per-user invariant enforced at both endpoints.
- [POST /uploadSharedPresentation](https://docs.slideless.ai/api-reference/upload-presentation): Create a new presentation. Body `{ title, html }` (≤ 10 MB), response `{ shareId, tokenId, token, shareUrl }`. CLI: `slideless share <path> --title "..."`.
- [POST /updateSharedPresentation](https://docs.slideless.ai/api-reference/update-presentation): Re-publish to an existing `shareId`. Body `{ shareId, html, title? }`, response `{ shareId, version, shareUrl }`. URL preserved, view count preserved, version auto-bumped. CLI: `slideless update <shareId> <path>`.
- [GET /listMyPresentations](https://docs.slideless.ai/api-reference/list-presentations): List user's presentations (cap 100, `createdAt desc`). CLI: `slideless list`.
- [GET /getSharedPresentationInfo/{shareId}](https://docs.slideless.ai/api-reference/get-presentation): Full metadata for one presentation including all tokens with per-token `accessCount` and `lastAccessedAt`. CLI: `slideless get <shareId>`.
- [GET /getSharedPresentation/{shareId}?token=…](https://docs.slideless.ai/api-reference/get-shared-presentation): Public, returns raw HTML, gated by token. Used by the Slideless viewer iframe internally. No CLI equivalent (in normal use you give recipients the share URL on `app.slideless.ai`).
- [POST /verifyApiKey](https://docs.slideless.ai/api-reference/verify-api-key): Validate a key without making destructive calls. CLI: `slideless verify` or `slideless whoami`.
